cards.front: What is a Linked List data structure?
cards.back: A linked list is a linear data structure where each element is a separate object, known as a node. Each node contains a value and a reference (or link) to the next node in the sequence.

cards.front: What is the purpose of a Stack data structure?
cards.back: A stack is a Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) data structure that follows the principle of Last In, First Out. It is used to implement recursive function calls, undo operations, and evaluating postfix expressions.

cards.front: What is the primary characteristic of a Queue data structure?
cards.back: A queue is a First-In-First-Out (FIFO) data structure that allows elements to be added to the end and removed from the front. It is commonly used in job scheduling, print queues, and network protocols.

cards.front: What is a Tree data structure used for?
cards.back: A tree is a hierarchical data structure consisting of nodes with a value and zero or more child nodes. Trees are used to represent file systems, database indexing, and organizational structures.

cards.front: What is the difference between a Set and a List data structure?
cards.back: A set is an unordered collection of unique elements, whereas a list is an ordered collection of elements that can be modified. Sets are used for membership testing and set operations, while lists are used for storing ordered data.
